title: erysimum humile subsp. neumannii
intro: Erysimum humile subsp. neumannii, also known as the dwarf wallflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, typically found in the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Erysimum humile subsp. neumannii lie in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Erysimum humile subsp. neumannii is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
